title: Windhawk Styler Mod Settings (Nested / DX Format)
description: A nested, human-friendly representation of Windhawk styler mod settings. Flatten to the Windhawk flat bracket-notation format with ConvertTo-WindhawkSettings.ps1.

styleConstants.description: Named token definitions. Each entry is a 'Name=Value' string. Reference tokens anywhere in controlStyles values using $Name (e.g. '$Background', '$Radius'). Flattens to styleConstants[0], styleConstants[1], ...

items.description: Format: 'Name=Value'. Name must be a valid identifier. Value is any scalar style value: hex color, named color, number, or thickness.

controlStyles.description: XAML element style rules keyed by target selector. Each key is a XAML target path; its value is an array of property assignment strings. Since all styles for a target live under one key, duplicates are naturally impossible. Flattens to controlStyles[N].target and controlStyles[N].styles[M] in key-insertion order.

additionalProperties.description: One or more XAML property assignments for this target. Flattens to controlStyles[N].styles[0], controlStyles[N].styles[1], etc.

resourceVariables.description: XAML resource dictionary overrides keyed by resource variable name. Each key is the resource name; its value is the replacement. Flattens to resourceVariables[N].variableKey and resourceVariables[N].value.

additionalProperties.description: Replacement value. Accepts a hex color (#AARRGGBB or #RRGGBB), named color, number, or thickness string.

xamlTarget.description: A XAML element selector path. Supports: qualified type names, namespace aliases (taskbar:, systemtray:), #Name IDs, [Attribute=Value] attribute selectors, > child combinators, [N] positional child index, and @VisualStateName on the target (scopes the entire rule to a parent element's visual state).

xamlStyleValue.description:
A XAML property assignment string. Four forms:
  (1) Scalar:       'Property=Value'
  (2) XAML object:  'Property:=<XamlElement ... />'
  (3) State-scoped: 'Property@VisualStateName=Value'
  (4) State+object: 'Property@VisualStateName:=<XamlElement ... />'
All forms accept $ConstantName references in the value position.

visualStateNames.description: Common visual state names for @State suffixes on both targets and style values.

xamlBrushTypes.WindhawkBlur: Fill:=<WindhawkBlur BlurAmount="20" TintColor="#80000000" />  — no named colors, no FallbackColor
xamlBrushTypes.LinearGradientBrush: Fill:=<LinearGradientBrush StartPoint="0,0.5" EndPoint="1,0.5"><GradientStop Color="Red" Offset="0" /><GradientStop Color="Blue" Offset="1" /></LinearGradientBrush>
